data.title: Analyst Maintains Sell Rating Despite Strong H1, Citing Unchanged Guidance, Program Delays and Weakening Order Backlog
data.description:
Goldman Sachs analyst Sam Burgess maintained a Sell rating on Dassault Aviation yesterday and set a price target of €300.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Sam Burgess has given his Sell rating due to a combination of factors, despite the strong first-half performance. He notes that while sales and profitability surprised positively, the unchanged full-year guidance signals limited upside, and management warns that 2026 seasonality may diverge from historic trends, adding uncertainty to the outlook.

He also highlights structural concerns, including the pushback of the Falcon 10X entry into service to 2029 and the decline in group order intake and backlog after last year’s large Rafale award. Together, these issues suggest weaker medium‑term growth visibility and constrain valuation support, leading Burgess to see the current share price as unattractive relative to the risk profile.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Ryan Merkel Reiterates Buy on James Hardie, Citing Strong Siding Volume Momentum Despite Near-Term Margin Pressure
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ryan Merkel has maintained their bullish stance on JHX stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ryan Merkel has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight James Hardie’s operational momentum and market opportunity. He notes that siding and trim revenue materially exceeded prior guidance, with implied double‑digit volume growth even as new residential construction remains soft, indicating effective execution on strategic initiatives and market share gains in repair and remodel.

Merkel also emphasizes that the upside in core siding volumes, supported by programs like Statement Essentials and aided by a competitor’s exit, sets a constructive tone for the fiscal year despite near‑term margin pressure, which he attributes in part to freight costs. While decking underperformed because of channel destocking, he views this as a temporary drag rather than a structural issue, and expects further clarity and potential estimate revisions after the full results on August 6, reinforcing his positive stance on the stock’s risk‑reward profile.

According to TipRanks, Merkel is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 4.1% and a 50.48% success rate. Merkel covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Ferguson PLC, Fastenal Company, and Aaon.

In another report released today, Truist Financial also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $24.78 price target.

data.title: Reliance Steel: Strong Q2 Results and Healthy Outlook Support Unchanged $379 Price Target and Hold Rating
data.description:
Goldman Sachs analyst Nick Cash maintained a Hold rating on Reliance Steel yesterday and set a price target of $379.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Nick Cash has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, starting with Reliance Steel’s very strong second-quarter performance and solid near-term outlook. Earnings, revenue, and EBITDA all exceeded expectations, driven by record shipments and favorable pricing, while guidance for the third quarter reflects broadly healthy demand across key end markets.

At the same time, Cash sees the current valuation as appropriately reflecting these strengths, which is why the $379 price target remains unchanged. He also highlights a balanced risk profile, noting that future returns could be influenced by shifts in costs, demand conditions, pricing trends, integration outcomes, and the pace of acquisitions, leading him to a neutral stance rather than a more aggressive Buy or Sell recommendation.

In another report released today, Wells Fargo also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $398.00 price target.

data.title: Rogers Communications: Buy Rating Reiterated as Fundamentals Improve and C$60 Price Target Remains Unchanged
data.description:
BMO Capital analyst Tim Casey maintained a Buy rating on Rogers Communication yesterday and set a price target of C$60.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Tim Casey has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Rogers Communications’ improving fundamentals and strategic positioning. The company delivered slightly better-than-expected revenue, EBITDA, and EPS in Q2, driven in part by a strong performance in its Media segment, while also reaffirming its 2026 outlook and keeping its C$60 price target unchanged.

Competitive dynamics in the wireless market are becoming more rational, supporting profitability, and capital intensity has dropped to its lowest level in years with further declines anticipated, which should accelerate deleveraging. In addition, Rogers’ plan to consolidate and partially sell down its sports and media assets is expected to unlock value and offset near-term leverage from the MLSE stake acquisition, reinforcing the Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, J.P. Morgan also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a C$65.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Weatherford Maintains Buy Rating as Analyst Trims Price Target to $110 on Strength in Margins and FCF Trajectory
data.description:
Analyst Phillip Jungwirth from BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating on Weatherford International and decreased the price target to $110.00 from $115.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Phillip Jungwirth has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Weatherford’s operating resilience and financial outlook. Despite known headwinds in the Middle East and the rolloff of a low-margin Saudi contract, the company delivered EBITDA above expectations, guided to improving margins, and is showing a stronger trajectory for free cash flow, even as he trims his official price target from $115 to $110.

Phillip Jungwirth’s rating is based on Weatherford’s attractive risk/reward profile, solid international positioning, and balance sheet strength. He sees the stock’s current valuation as compelling relative to its peers, underpinned by durable margin performance, rising FCF conversion, and exposure to secular growth drivers such as energy security spending, offshore and deepwater activity, and a future rebound in Middle East markets, all of which support a positive long-term investment case.

In another report released yesterday, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $154.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on West as Biologics-Driven Double-Digit Growth, Margin Upside and Conservative Outlook Signal Further Upside Potential
data.description:
William Blair analyst Matt Larew has maintained their bullish stance on WST stock, giving a Buy rating on July 15.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Matt Larew has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including West’s ability to deliver another quarter of solid double-digit organic growth, driven largely by strong momentum in its biologics franchise. He highlights that both GLP and non-GLP products are contributing meaningfully, with non-GLP offerings growing at a high-teens rate and GLP-1–related products already representing a notable share of total company revenue.

Larew also points to the company’s top-line results, which exceeded both his and the Street’s expectations, as well as the improved margins and earnings per share performance that underscore operating leverage. In his view, the revised full-year outlook retains an element of conservatism despite a strong first half, leaving room for upside if demand for West’s elastomer and West Vantage solutions continues to track ahead of plan.

According to TipRanks, Larew is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 10.0% and a 53.13% success rate. Larew covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as West Pharmaceutical Services, Option Care Health, and Repligen.

In another report released on July 15, TD Cowen also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $410.00 price target.
